name: backend-integration-adapter
purpose: Runtime adapter contract for identity, product loading, Stripe checkout, payment verification, and post-purchase account flow.

storageScope.reason: Each browser tab is treated as a separate Web2App session. A new tab or an explicit Start new plan action creates a fresh visitor context.
forbidden: anonymous identity creation on entry page
anonymous identity creation on start CTA

steps: save answer to React state and sessionStorage
disable selected option or CTA and show loading
navigate only after identity and Firestore write succeed
firstSubmissionPolicy.failureBehavior: stay on the same page with a retryable error

tokenExchange.warning: customToken must not be sent as Billing API bearer token

sessionPolicy.startNewPlan: clear current tab session keys, sign out Firebase Auth, reset committedPhase to entry, then route to the first OB question
sessionPolicy.protectedPages: payment_success, account_create, account, subscription management, and cancellation require an existing session identity; if missing, route to login or entry instead of creating anonymous identity
sessionPolicy.paywallCheckout: requires the session identity that should have been created after the first OB answer; direct paywall access without identity must not silently create a new anonymous user

resolveOffers.placementCodeSource: URL query lpid first, then explicit run config placementCode

frontendRecommended.placementCodeSource: URL query lpid or explicit run config
frontendRecommended.warning: placementCode/lpid must not be used as paywallCode. /products and /paywalls/{paywallCode}/* are internal/debug fallback endpoints only.

createEmbeddedSession.warning: visitor must be true or false. Do not put answers or attribution inside visitor; answers are persisted to Firestore by uid and attribution uses its dedicated endpoint when configured.

verification.paymentVerificationPolicy: api-reference.md exposes subscription status, subscription list, and entitlements as the frontend verification surface. Do not require payments/by-provider-session unless a later API reference adds it.

subscriptionManagement.cancelSubscription: not currently exposed in inputs/api-reference.md; UI may show a management placeholder or external support path until backend provides a frontend cancel endpoint

fallbackPolicy.productApiFailure: Use API-ready mock plans with visible development status.
fallbackPolicy.identityFailure: Block authenticated checkout and show a recoverable error.
fallbackPolicy.answerPersistenceFailure: Keep answers in runtime state/session storage. First-answer persistence failure must stay on the same page with retry; later Firestore failures must not block OB progress.
fallbackPolicy.subscriptionLoadFailure: Show a recoverable account-page error and keep the user logged in.
fallbackPolicy.cancelSubscriptionFailure: Do not claim cancellation is available unless inputs/api-reference.md exposes a frontend cancel endpoint.
fallbackPolicy.checkoutFailure: Do not fake payment success when backend is configured.
fallbackPolicy.mockMode: Allowed only when the run explicitly declares mock mode or no backend API reference is provided.

answerPersistence.trigger: after first-answer identity is available, and after each later answer/snapshot
answerPersistence.failurePolicy: first answer blocks until Firestore succeeds; later writes do not block OB and may be retried by the app if implemented
